did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper is a review of Fluorescence Lifetime Imaging Microscopy (FLIM) for skin cancer diagnosis. It discusses physical principles, instrumentation (e.g., two-photon microscopy), and clinical applications based on fluorescence lifetime measurements and phasor analysis. It does not report the use of AI, machine learning, or deep learning algorithms to obtain results; the methods described are optical and biophysical.

non_compliance_reason: The paper does not use AI to obtain results in dermatology.
